target_hints: Waits for the root + status badges to exist.

Waits for the example root to exist.
Toggles the panel via shortcut.
Disables availability and asserts the shortcut no longer toggles.
Waits a short time for image registration to settle.
Captures a baseline screenshot + bundle for alpha compositing semantics.
Waits for CustomV1 support + registration semantics.
Toggles the effect on/off and adjusts a strength preset.
Captures a screenshot + diagnostics bundle.
Waits for the root + table slot to exist.
Captures a baseline screenshot + bundle.
Waits for the root to exist, then captures a baseline screenshot + bundle.
Waits for the example root to exist.
Toggles Pixelate/Blur/None and captures screenshots + bundle.
Clicks inside the embedded viewport surface to verify explicit input forwarding.
Switches the render target size preset and verifies the target size gate updates.
Captures a screenshot + diagnostics bundle.
Verifies an imported render target is visible via ViewportSurface.
Switches the target size preset and asserts numeric semantics update.
Captures a screenshot + diagnostics bundle.
Waits for the root to exist.
Performs a simple pointer drag + wheel interaction over the viewport.
Verifies the position-length numeric semantics are present (reset returns to ~0).
Captures a screenshot + bundle.
Waits for the hello example root to exist.
Captures a baseline screenshot + bundle.
Waits for the counter view to exist.
Clicks increment and captures a screenshot + bundle.
Validates that icon + asset panels mount and that the reload epoch button is wired.
Waits briefly for the first upload to settle.
Captures a baseline screenshot + bundle.
Invokes the command palette, clicks a declarative button, a GenUI-rendered button, a GenUI dropdown menu item, and an imui button that dispatch the same ActionId.
Asserts all triggers update the same view-local state via a shared typed action handler.
Opens the dialog via the trigger.
Clicks the close affordance.
Waits for the content node to disappear.
Captures a screenshot + bundle.
Clicks the dialog trigger.
Waits for the content node to exist.
Captures a screenshot + bundle.
Waits for the root to exist.
Waits for the query to reach Success and show stable demo text.
Captures a baseline screenshot + bundle.
Waits for the root node to exist.
Navigates to Settings and back.
Captures a screenshot + diagnostics bundle.
Waits for the example root to exist.
Captures Light and Dark screenshots + bundle.
Closes the controls panel via a harness button and asserts controls-only widgets disappear.
Also asserts the resulting dock graph canonical signature.
Closes the left viewport panel via a harness button and asserts the viewport node disappears.
Closes the right viewport panel via a harness button and asserts the viewport node disappears.
Also asserts the resulting dock graph canonical signature.
Asserts the default dock graph shape for the docking arbitration demo (dockview-style).
This gate intentionally does not include split fractions/shares.
Drag a docked tab to the float-zone affordance (top-right of the dock tab bar) and drop it.
Expected behavior: the panel becomes an in-window floating container (no OS window tear-off).
This gate is invariant-based (dock diagnostics), not pixel-based.
Create an in-window floating container via float-zone, then drag its title bar far outside the window.
Expected behavior: the floating rect is clamped to window bounds (ImGui-style hand feel) and the drag is observed by diagnostics.
Create an in-window floating container via float-zone, then drag its title bar onto docking hint rectangles.
Expected behavior: while dragging, docking hint routing is published and on drop the floating container merges back into the main dock tree (ImGui-style).
Injects an IncomingOpenRequest event and asserts it is observable via event_kind_seen.
This gates that the app driver records platform-delivered events into the diagnostics ring buffer.
Opens a popover and asserts docking drag closes it (capture), then opens a dialog and asserts underlay click does not leak through.
This script exercises an opt-in behavior: creating multiple DockFloating OS windows without merging the first one back.
It tears off two distinct tabs (left + right) from the main window, then drags them back one-by-one and asserts the dock graph is canonical, has no floatings, and still contains the baseline panels.
Note: cursor overrides are expressed in window-client physical pixels.

This script exercises an ImGui-style continuous tab tear-off: start dragging a tab, allow tear-off to create a floating OS window, then route the same drag back into the main window without releasing.
It relies on schema v2 window-targeted steps to migrate the active script across windows.
Note: cursor overrides are expressed in window-client logical pixels (unless a step explicitly requests physical).
This gate is a stress variant of the multi-window drag-back flow.
After tear-off creates a floating OS window, the script moves that floating window far along +X (cross-monitor if present).
Expected behavior: cross-window drag routing remains correct and the tab can still be docked back into the main window (no coordinate/DPI drift regressions).

Evidence: the script captures bundles both right after tear-off and after the large outer move; use `fretboard diag dock-routing <bundle_dir>` to inspect dock-drag routing (including scale_factor_x1000 fields when available).
Note: if the host only has a single monitor, the OS may clamp the outer position; the gate still exercises large-coordinate paths and must pass.
Tears off a tab into a dock-floating OS window.
Starts a cross-window drag and sweeps the inner 5-way docking hint pad in the main window (top/left/right/bottom/center).
Asserts that each hint can be selected deterministically via `dock_drop_resolved_zone_is`.
This script overlaps two OS windows and asserts that cross-window dock drag hover routing follows window z-order when the cursor is in the overlap region.
It raises each window without moving the cursor and expects `dock_drag_current_window_is` to switch accordingly (ImGui-style).
Cursor overrides use window-client logical coordinates to stay stable under DPI scaling.
This script gates a Windows-specific fallback: finishing a cross-window dock drag when the left mouse release is not delivered to any window.
It starts a cross-window tab drag, intentionally omits pointer_up, then uses a diagnostics-only mouse-button override to make the runner believe the left button is down while the OS reports it as up.
The runner should detect this mismatch and finish the drag using cursor-based drop routing (ImGui-style).
This script repeatedly tears off a tab to create a floating OS window, then drags it back into the main window.
Expected behavior: after each cycle the dock graph returns to the default canonical signature without deepening splits or growing node count.
Note: cursor overrides are expressed in window-client physical pixels.
This gate merges left+right viewports into one tab strip, tears off the whole tab stack via a tabs-group drag, then docks it back into the main window via a title-bar (tabs-group) drag.
Expected behavior: the floating OS window auto-closes after the drop, and the main window still contains left+right+controls with no floatings.
This gate exercises an ImGui-like title-bar docking gesture (custom chrome):
1) tear off a tab to create a floating OS window, then release,
2) start a *tabs-group* drag from empty tab-bar space (treated as a title-bar grab),
3) dock the floating OS window back into the main window and auto-close the floating window.
It captures bundles at 'drop' and 'after-close' to isolate docking-apply vs window-close cleanup regressions.
This gate overlaps two OS windows and starts a cross-window dock tab drag in the topmost window.
Expected (transparent payload): hover routing peeks behind the moving window so docking previews/resolve can target the overlapped window.
This mirrors ImGui terminology: `HoveredWindowUnderMovingWindow` becomes the effective hover target when NoInputs/click-through behavior is desired.
This gate overlaps two OS windows and starts a cross-window tabs-group dock drag in the topmost window.
Expected (transparent payload): hover routing peeks behind the moving window so docking previews/resolve can target the overlapped window.
It differs from the basic under-moving-window gate by starting the drag on empty tab-bar space (tabs-group drag), not on a specific tab (panel drag).
This script starts a tab drag, asserts the docking preview becomes active, then presses Escape mid-drag and asserts the drag session is no longer active.
This script drags the splitter far beyond the window bounds in both directions.
Expected behavior: the adjacent viewport panels clamp at the docking policy minimum size and never collapse.
Run with env: FRET_DOCK_ARB_PRESET=large
Optional: FRET_DOCK_ARB_NO_PERSIST=1 to avoid writing .fret/layout.json
Run with env: FRET_DOCK_ARB_PRESET=large
Recommended: FRET_DOCK_ARB_DISALLOW_DROP_TARGETS=1 (avoid layout mutation on drop)
Optional: FRET_DOCK_ARB_NO_PERSIST=1 to avoid writing .fret/layout.json
Builds a long tab stack by merging multiple single-tab viewports into one leaf, then asserts drop-at-end resolves insert_index == tab_count.
This gate avoids pixel-based tab rect assumptions; it relies on docking interaction diagnostics.
This script first merges two viewports into a single tab stack, then asserts drop-at-end resolves insert_index == 2.
It cancels the final drag (Escape) to avoid mutating layout beyond the setup step.
Starts a tab drag on the first tab, then parks the pointer at the right edge of the active tab bar.
Gates that docking diagnostics report `tab_strip_active_visibility.scroll` increasing (edge auto-scroll).
Uses a semantics-only anchor positioned over an inactive tab's close affordance.
Asserts the active tab index does not change on pointer-down, then releases to close.
Opens the overflow menu and presses the row-1 close affordance.
Asserts no dock drag/floating drag is started.
Asserts the active tab index does not change while closing the overflowed tab.
Builds a long tab stack by merging multiple single-tab viewports into one leaf, then opens the overflow menu and selects row 1.
Uses non-hit-test semantics anchors so pointer injection targets real docking chrome.
Count sweep point for attributing macOS (graphics) unmapped footprint vs image pressure.

fret-ui-gallery
fret-ui-gallery
Resets diagnostics so dispatch trace assertions are deterministic.
Targets the already-active tab (doc-a-2) so the close target is in-view and deterministic.
Clicks the close button and asserts a pointer-triggered dispatch trace entry exists.
Asserts the tab is removed from the UI tree.
Activates doc-a-1 (pane A) so MRU is deterministic.
Closes doc-a-1 via the close button.
Asserts doc-a-1 is removed and selection falls back to doc-a-2 (MRU fallback).
Activates a different tab so the drag source tab is inactive.
Drags the inactive tab toward pane B's right edge until the split preview appears.
Drops to split and asserts the tab moved into the new pane.
Drags a tab from pane A toward the right edge of pane B.
Waits for the right-edge drop preview indicator to appear.
Captures a screenshot while the preview is visible (used to gate docking drop overlay tokens).
Starts a pointer session on an active tab.
Pins the cursor inside the window edge and asserts the right split preview appears.
Drops and asserts the preview disappears and the tab moves into the new split pane.
Drag a tab from pane A over pane B while staying in pane B's tab strip row.
Asserts the right-edge split preview does not activate until the pointer leaves the tab strip row.
Waits for a known tab in pane A to exist.
Middle-clicks the tab.
Asserts the tab is removed from the UI tree (not_exists).
Shrinks the window to force tab strip overflow.
Opens the overflow menu.
Selects a known tab entry from the overflow list.
Asserts the tab becomes selected.
Opens preview A, then toggles pin on the active tab via demo-only debug controls.
Asserts pinning commits the preview (preview marker removed, pinned marker present).
Pins doc-a-0, then drags doc-a-1 onto the pinned tab surface.
Asserts the unpinned tab is not promoted to pinned.
Opens preview A, commits it, then opens preview B.
Asserts the committed preview remains as a normal tab and the new preview is marked as preview.
Clicks the demo-only debug controls to open preview A, then preview B.
Asserts the previous preview tab is replaced (removed) and the new one is marked as preview.
Shrinks the window to force tab strip overflow.
Drags the first tab onto the explicit end-drop surface.
Asserts the tab becomes the last tab (pos_in_set == set_size).
Asserts initial pos_in_set for a known tab.
Drags the first tab to the end and drops it.
Asserts pos_in_set updates after the reorder.
